archetype.quote: You operate at the top — broad machinery, well used. Your thinnest axis is breadth — that's where the next gain is.
archetype.title: Elite

growth_edges.advice: You're even across the build sprint, so the next gear isn't a weak spot to patch — it's depth. Add a short retro after each session and let the learnings compound session over session. (gstack names this /retro — the Reflect stage.)
growth_edges.title: You're balanced — your edge is depth

engineering.axis_narrative: Engineering scores 8.8/10 (excellent). Strongest: Quality ceremony (100%); weakest: Low errors (70%).

engineering.drag_note: Low errors is dragging this down — 3 errors/100 tools (target ≤10).

signature_moves.evidence: 80 code-review passes — one of your most-used skills. You don't trust a diff until a second set of eyes has seen it.
signature_moves.title: You review more than you write

signature_moves.evidence: 230 delegated & backgrounded agent runs. You parallelize and grind rather than babysit one chat.
signature_moves.title: You run a team, not a tool

signature_moves.evidence: 600 reasoning blocks (~15/session) before edits land — you deliberate hard, then commit.
signature_moves.title: You think before you touch the diff

signature_moves.evidence: The agent stopped to ask you on just 2% of 300 prompts — you point it and let it run, rather than getting pulled into a back-and-forth.
signature_moves.title: You direct, you don't deliberate

signature_moves.evidence: You opened 16 of 40 sessions with a plan — you scaffold the decision before the implementation, gstack-style.
signature_moves.title: You write the plan before the code

steering.detail: ~13 actions per turn before you weigh in · the agent checked in on 2% of your prompts
steering.gloss: you point the agent and let it run
steering.label: Long leash

signals.grounded_session_rule: knowledge-MCP call OR explore-class project/data/design MCP call before a later Edit/Write/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit in the same session
signals.score_formula: coverage = grounded_sessions / write_sessions; score = min(1, coverage / 0.40)

archetype.quote: Still developing — the habits aren't compounding yet. Your thinnest axis is breadth — that's where the next gain is.
archetype.title: Apprentice

growth_edges.advice: 40× on one file and 5 files past 15 edits, next to ~5.0 errors per 100 tool calls — that pairing reads as retry-thrash more than deliberate iteration. When a file resists past ~15 tries, find the root cause before the next edit. (gstack names this /investigate.)
growth_edges.title: When a file fights back, root-cause it

growth_edges.advice: Planning is 3.1. Sketch the plan and reframe the ask before writing code — it's the cheapest place to catch a wrong turn. (gstack front-loads this with /office-hours + /autoplan.)
growth_edges.title: Spend more time in Think + Plan

growth_edges.advice: Engineering is 3.9. Add one deliberate review-and-test pass on every branch before you ship — that's where craft compounds. (gstack's back half: /review, /qa, /investigate, /retro.)
growth_edges.title: Run a quality pass before you ship

signature_moves.evidence: 230 delegated & backgrounded agent runs. You parallelize and grind rather than babysit one chat.
signature_moves.title: You run a team, not a tool

signature_moves.evidence: 600 reasoning blocks (~15/session) before edits land — you deliberate hard, then commit.

signals.grounded_session_rule: knowledge-MCP call OR explore-class project/data/design MCP call before a later Edit/Write/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit in the same session
signals.score_formula: coverage = grounded_sessions / write_sessions; score = min(1, coverage / 0.40)
